chapter_title: About Being tolerant in Business Transactions

hadith_entries.text:
748. Narrated by Jaber ibn Abdullah: 'Allah's Messenger
said: "May Allah's Mercy be on him who is lenient in his
buying, selling, and in demanding back his money."
(Bukhari)

749. Narrated Abu-Horaira : 'The Prophet said:
"There was a merchant who used to lend the people, and
whenever his debtor was under straitened circumstances, he
would say to his employees: 'Grant him a period of grace so
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)
that Allah will forgive us.' So, Allah forgave him."
(Bukhari)

750. Narrated Abu-Huraira: 'The Messenger of Allah *
said: "He who gives a grace period to an insolvent or remitted
his debt (in whole or in part), Allah would take him under his
shade on the Day of Judgment when there will be no shade
except His." (At-Termizi)

751. Narrated Sowayd ibn Qays: 'Makhrafa Al-Abdi and I
imported some linen garments from Hajar. The Prophet
came to us and bought some pants. I have had a hired weigher
to whom the Messenger of Allah said: "Give full and fair
weight."" (At-Termizi)
Hajar is a city in Yemen or a suburb of Madina.
Riyadhos-Saliheen (The Meadows of the Righteous)
(۱۲) كتاب العلم
Book of Knowledge
